creator: w-info@xs4all.nl
text:
> From: 	Mark Linimon <linimon@lonesome.com>
> To: 	bug-followup@FreeBSD.org
> Date: 	Sat, 21 Jan 2012 13:14:18 -0600
> ----- Forwarded message from Marius Strobl <marius@alchemy.franken.de> -----
>
> Date: Sat, 21 Jan 2012 17:38:42 +0100
> From: Marius Strobl <marius@alchemy.franken.de>
> To: freebsd-ports-bugs@FreeBSD.org
> Subject: Re: ports/164332: growisofs/cdrecord error on 9.0R (unable to
> CAMGETPASSTHRU for /dev/cd0)
>
> You seem to be doing something wrong. 9.0-RELEASE includes ATA_CAM by
> default so you don't need atapicam. Also make sure that the ports are
> actually compiled on 9.0-RELEASE. There was an ABI breakage in libcam
> rather late in the release cycle which could lead to even the shipped
> packages to not include binaries that actually work on 9.0-RELEASE.
>
> ----- End forwarded message -----


Aha, yes, that's what I meant, the packages cdrtools/dvd+rwtools don't 
work in 9.0R.

I had atapicam autoloaded from the config I'd copied from my older FBSD 
install, so unloaded it.

I then compiled from ports, and they both work. I read that there were 
late SCSI layer changes but I expected the packages to be compiled after 
that and thus working ;-)

So, thanks! But shouldn't the packages dependent on those changes be 
replaced? I suppose others will run into the same problem. I usually get 
the packages as it's much faster than compiling myself, at least for 
really big compiles.

Regards,

Wouter
